title: algorithm() returning Result<> is correct even if inconvenient
description: Some signing backends (PKCS#11, Vault) need a network call to determine the key algorithm. Returning SigningAlgorithm directly would force the return type to be infallible and callers to unwrap silently. Returning Result<SigningAlgorithm, SecretError> is the honest type. A contributor might suggest splitting into two traits or adding a fixed_algorithm() convenience method — fine additions, but the base trait must be Result.
